text.title: Freelance Pilot Pricing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Freelance Pilot Pricing. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pilot price MUST be at least 1.0x cost basis (delivery cost incl. tools + your time at minimum acceptable rate). Below-cost pilots are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Below-cost pilots subsidize discovery and create unsustainable expectations.

rule.statement: Pilot timeline MUST be ≤14 calendar days end-to-end. Pilots that need longer are not pilots — they are projects.
rule.rationale: Timebox enforces fixed scope and surfaces fit fast.

rule.statement: Pilot price includes a public case-study clause; consent (logo + numeric outcome publishable) MUST be captured in writing before pilot starts.
rule.rationale: Verbal consent lapses; written consent is the actual deliverable that justifies the discount.

rule.statement: Pilot agreement MUST state the post-pilot standard price + transition trigger (renewal date OR continuation past timebox).
rule.rationale: Unstated transition leads to forever-pilot-price retention; document up front.

rule.statement: Maximum 5 pilots before the offering transitions to standard price for new customers. Existing pilots may continue at pilot price until their stated transition.
rule.rationale: Beyond 5 case studies, the discount is no longer justified by proof-of-concept need.
